ps140: More radiantly luminous than the sun,* your memory has shone forth upon the faithful,* enlightening creation of divine splendours, O most blessed Simeon,* and dispelling the gloom of the passions and the darkness of the demons.* Wherefore, we call you blessed* and prayerfully hymn you as a beacon for the whole world* and a fervent intercessor.

O wise martyr, having acquired heavenly things through the things of earth,* and permanent things through that which is transitory,* rejoicing, you received incorrupt glory through corruptible things,* and patiently, through tortures and wounds for the Word of God,* you obtained crowns and the kingdom,* wherein, rejoicing, O glorious one, with those who suffered with you,* pray for all who praise you with faith.

The shafts of your words wounded the assemblies of the iniquitous, O martyr Simeon;* which caused the clouds of heaven to lift up their voice* and the choirs of the incorporeal ones to come together in joy,* marvelling at the patience of your struggles.* Wherefore, rejoicing, we honour with faith your holy repose,* glorifying the Saviour.

As a right acceptable hierarch,* you behold the noetic tabernacles* and have been vouchsafed to see never-waning and heavenly sights,* arrayed in holiness, clad in righteousness and magnificence;* and, face to face, O blessed one,* you behold the beauty of noetic things.

O venerable father Acacius,* furnishing your mind with wings to fly to God with faith,* you disdained the disorderly state of the world* and, taking up your cross,* you followed after Him who beholds all things,* enslaving to your mind the intemperance of your body* through feats of asceticism,* by the power of the divine Spirit.

Shown to be a partisan of the godly Council,* preeminent with Cyril, O Acacius,* you committed the wicked Nestorius to damnation,* denouncing his mindlessness, O blessed one,* and putting his vanity to shame with spiritual discourse.* Wherefore, every soul calls you blessed,* celebrating with faith your divine memory.
theotokion: Save me, O all-pure Mistress,* who ineffably gave birth to Christ the Saviour,* for you alone have I acquired as an intercessor,* my insuperable rampart, my protection and joy,* the divine consolation of my soul.* Wherefore, deliver me from the worm that sleeps not* and from everlasting fire, O Mother of Christ God.
stavrotheotokion: Beholding You crucified upon the Cross, O Jesus,* accepting suffering willingly,* Your Virgin Mother cried out:* “Woe is me, O Master, my sweet Child!* How is it that You unjustly endure wounds,* O Physician who has healed the weakness of men* and delivered all from corruption by Your lovingkindness?"
